lede: A look at November 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Marie Curie and James Cook, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: NASA
events.desc: NASA launches the Mars Global Surveyor.

events.title: Magic Johnson
events.desc: Magic Johnson announces that he is HIV-positive and retires from the NBA.

events.title: Mass Rapid Transit (Singapore)
events.desc: The Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) system in Singapore opens for passenger service.

events.title: Bangladesh
events.desc: In Bangladesh, a joint force of people and soldiers takes part in an uprising led by Colonel Abu Taher that ousts and kills Brigadier Khaled Mosharraf, freeing the then house-arrested army chief and future president Major General Ziaur Rahman.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Soviet hospital ship Armenia is sunk by German planes while evacuating refugees and wounded military and staff of several Crimean hospitals. It is estimated that over 5,000 people died in the sinking.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Third Battle of Gaza ends, with British forces capturing Gaza from the Ottoman Empire.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Belmont: In Belmont, Missouri, Union forces led by General Ulysses S. Grant overrun a Confederate camp but are forced to retreat when Confederate reinforcements arrive.

events.title: Tecumseh's War
events.desc: Tecumseh's War: The Battle of Tippecanoe is fought near present-day Battle Ground, Indiana, United States.

events.title: O Ewigkeit, du Donnerwort, BWV 60
events.desc: O Ewigkeit, du Donnerwort, BWV 60, a dialogue cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach for Leipzig, was first performed.

events.title: Third Council of Constantinople
events.desc: The Sixth Ecumenical Council commences in Constantinople.

births.name: Marie Curie
births.line: Polish chemist and physicist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Ögedei Khan
births.line: Mongol ruler, 2nd Great Khan of the Mongol Empire

births.name: David Guetta
births.line: French DJ, record producer, remixer, and songwriter

births.name: David Petraeus
births.line: American general, Director of the Central Intelligence Agency

births.name: Leon Trotsky
births.line: Russian theorist and politician, founded the Red Army

deaths.text: Rudolf Augstein, German journalist, co-founded Der Spiegel

observances.text: Christian feast day: All Dominican Saints and Blesseds
Christian feast day: Engelbert II of Berg
Christian feast day: John Christian Frederick Heyer (Lutheran)
Christian feast day: Vicente Liem de la Paz (one of Vietnamese Martyrs)
